gates.purpose: Define the non-executable boundary for moving from pre-adaptation review into adapted-output drafting for the first Skills MVP candidate batch.
preconditions_before_adaptation: The user must explicitly approve creation of adapted output for this candidate batch.
The candidate source, revision, and upstream hashes must still match the recorded batch and review evidence.
License and provenance posture must remain reviewed and unresolved gaps must fail closed.
The work must stay in non-runtime review or staging surfaces until separate release approval.
Existing curated Skills, recipes, native/runtime capabilities, and conflict groups must remain the comparison baseline.
allowed_after_explicit_approval: Create adapted draft text in a non-runtime review surface.
Record candidate-specific disposition as merge, recipe-only, adapter-only, reject, or approved-payload candidate.
Run focused security, portability, overlap, attribution, and validation review on the actual adapted output.
Prepare release-candidate evidence only after the adapted output has passed review.
disallowed_without_approval: Do not update generated routing projections as executable routes.
Do not install, restore, or sync any candidate into live Agent environments.
Do not redistribute upstream source text as curated payload.
Do not claim any candidate is approved or released.
fail_closed_conditions: No explicit human approval is recorded.
Source revision or upstream hash differs from recorded evidence.
License, provenance, attribution, or redistribution posture is unclear.
Adaptation would override repository, human, or runtime authority.
Adaptation would introduce vendor-specific, agent-specific, or project-specific assumptions as universal rules.
Overlap with existing curated Skills, official/runtime capabilities, or recipes is unresolved.
The change attempts to enter release manifest, routing projection, or live environment before release approval.
acceptance_to_leave_gate: Explicit human approval for adapted-output creation is recorded.
Adapted draft output exists only in an approved non-runtime review surface.
Security, portability, overlap, attribution, and validation evidence is recorded for the adapted output.
Repository verification passes without granting runtime, manifest, routing, or live-install status prematurely.

required_decisions: merge existing PRD, issue, TDD, and review guidance versus recipe-only
keep command examples repository-local and non-default
separate portable ADR lifecycle guidance from repository-local documentation standards
merge into existing review and quality Skills versus recipe-only
preserve repository and human merge authority
avoid making heavy review mandatory for trivial or low-risk tasks
